title: Handle the API Failure
constraints: The agent must retry failed API calls up to 3 times
The agent must inform the user if all retries fail
You may not change the simulated API behavior
The final response must still be helpful even if some data is unavailable
hints: Wrap the tool call in error handling that catches exceptions and retries
Consider adding a fallback message when the API is completely unavailable
In LangGraph, you can create a retry loop as a conditional edge that checks for errors and routes back to the tool-calling node

starterCode.langchain:
import random
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_tool_calling_agent
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate
from langchain_core.tools import tool

@tool
def fetch_stock_price(ticker: str) -> str:
    """Fetch the current stock price for a given ticker symbol."""
    # Simulated unreliable API - fails 60% of the time
    if random.random() < 0.6:
        raise ConnectionError(f"API timeout: Could not reach stock service for {ticker}")
    return f"{ticker}: $142.50 (+2.3%)"

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "You are a financial assistant. Help users check stock prices."),
    ("human", "{input}"),
    ("placeholder", "{agent_scratchpad}"),
])

# BUG: No error handling - agent crashes on API failure
agent = create_tool_calling_agent(llm, [fetch_stock_price], prompt)
executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=[fetch_stock_price])

result = executor.invoke({"input": "What's the current price of AAPL?"})
print(result["output"])
starterCode.crewai:
import random
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai.tools import tool

@tool
def fetch_stock_price(ticker: str) -> str:
    """Fetch the current stock price for a given ticker symbol."""
    # Simulated unreliable API - fails 60% of the time
    if random.random() < 0.6:
        raise ConnectionError(f"API timeout: Could not reach stock service for {ticker}")
    return f"{ticker}: $142.50 (+2.3%)"

# BUG: No error handling - agent crashes on API failure
assistant = Agent(
    role="Financial Assistant",
    goal="Help users check stock prices",
    backstory="You are a financial assistant.",
    tools=[fetch_stock_price],
)

task = Task(
    description="What's the current price of AAPL?",
    expected_output="The current stock price of AAPL",
    agent=assistant,
)

crew = Crew(agents=[assistant], tasks=[task])
result = crew.kickoff()
print(result)
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
import random
from agents import Agent, Runner
from agents.tool import function_tool

@function_tool
def fetch_stock_price(ticker: str) -> str:
    """Fetch the current stock price for a given ticker symbol."""
    # Simulated unreliable API - fails 60% of the time
    if random.random() < 0.6:
        raise ConnectionError(f"API timeout: Could not reach stock service for {ticker}")
    return f"{ticker}: $142.50 (+2.3%)"

# BUG: No error handling - agent crashes on API failure
agent = Agent(
    name="Financial Assistant",
    instructions="You are a financial assistant. Help users check stock prices.",
    tools=[fetch_stock_price],
)

result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "What's the current price of AAPL?")
print(result.final_output)

evaluationCriteria.label: Implements retry logic
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent retries the API call up to 3 times before giving up

evaluationCriteria.label: Graceful failure message
evaluationCriteria.description: If all retries fail, the agent informs the user clearly instead of crashing

evaluationCriteria.label: Succeeds when API recovers
evaluationCriteria.description: When the API works on a retry attempt, the agent returns the data normally

evaluationCriteria.label: No unhandled exceptions
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent never throws an unhandled exception to the user
